excerpt: Sincerity (ikhlāṣ) denotes action undertaken without regard to worldly gain or personal benefit, the performance of duty for its own sake, as an offering to the Divine.
source_location_hint: On sincerity and purity of action

excerpt: The Commander of the Faithful ʿAlī ibn Abī Ṭālib expressed that he worships Allah not for desire of Paradise nor fear of Hell, but because Allah is worthy of worship.
source_authority: Attributed to ʿAlī via Ahmad ibn ʿAbd Allah

excerpt: Sincerity arises when the believer establishes firm conviction that he possesses no power (ḥawl) or strength (quwwa) over anything whatsoever, and pronounces: 'There is neither cunning nor strength save in Allah the Most High, the Magnificent.'
source_location_hint: On the removal of ḥawl and quwwa as condition of sincerity

excerpt: The Commander of the Faithful declared: 'We are content with the decree of the Mighty One concerning us: He has given us knowledge, and to our enemies, wealth.'
source_authority: Attributed to ʿAlī via Ahmad ibn ʿAbd Allah
source_location_hint: On trust in Allah and acceptance of divine decree

excerpt: Allah declares: 'And endure you patiently (O Muhammad), your patience is not but from Allah. And grieve not over them, and be not distressed because of what they plot.'
source_location_hint: Qur'ān 16:127 on patience as divine gift

excerpt: For the truly patient, Allah proclaims: 'And obey Allah and His Messenger, and do not dispute lest you lose courage and your strength depart, and be patient. Surely, Allah is with those who are patient.'
source_location_hint: Qur'ān 8:46 on Allah's presence with the patient

excerpt: There exist three grades of patience and love for Allah. The first two are metaphorical love; the third alone constitutes true love.
source_location_hint: On the three grades of patience

excerpt: The story of the blind and paralyzed elder who praised Allah despite his afflictions, demonstrating that even extraordinary patience may be surpassed by those who rejoice in hardship for Allah's sake.
source_location_hint: Story of Dhū'l-Nūn al-Miṣrī and the elder

excerpt: The Prophet alluded to the distinction between trials: 'The believer is tested; the disbeliever is punished.'
source_authority: Prophetic tradition via Ahmad ibn ʿAbd Allah
source_location_hint: On the purpose of affliction for believer and disbeliever

excerpt: Prophet Solomon, when granted dominion and taught the language of beasts and birds, declared: 'This is a favor from my Lord, that He may test me whether I am grateful or ungrateful.'
source_location_hint: Qur'ān 27:40 on Solomon's gratitude for divine favor

excerpt: The Prophet (peace be upon him) said: 'If patience were created as a human being, he would be made into a righteous man.'
source_authority: Prophetic tradition via Ahmad ibn ʿAbd Allah

excerpt: Maulana ʿAlī has said: the ascetic is one who neither rejoices when worldly blessing comes to him nor grieves when it departs. Living in the world while renouncing worldly attachment is true asceticism.
The Messenger of Allah said: 'Whoever turns away from the world in asceticism—the world holds him in contempt.'
source_authority: Prophetic tradition via Ahmad ibn ʿAbd Allah

excerpt: The purification of the soul (zakāt al-nafs) refers to the capacity to receive something good within one's self and the capacity to reject evil. The root of all blessed blessings of Allah is this foundation, upon which all other blessings rest.
source_location_hint: On the purification and perfection of the soul

excerpt: Allah declares: 'And those who have been bestowed with knowledge and faith will say: Indeed you have stayed according to the Decree of Allah, until the Day of Resurrection.'
source_location_hint: Qur'ān 30:56 on those given both knowledge and faith
